Avoma call 22 May 2026, Tanya (demo): 'disorganized intake — procurement requests coming via email, WhatsApp, Teams messages'; 'tail spend inefficiency — no systematic negotiation'; 'ERP gap — IBM Maximo backend not connected'
metrics.narrative: Ammar articulated operational pain on the 22 May call: (1) disorganized intake ('procurement requests coming via email, WhatsApp, Teams messages with no structure'), (2) no guided buying ('users don't know policies, approved vendors, or who to escalate to'), (3) tail spend inefficiency ('no systematic negotiation for high-frequency, lower-value items'), and (4) ERP gap ('IBM Maximo backend not connected to procurement front-end'). Ammar confirmed TAQA is 'asset core company dealing with billions and strategic spares' (verbatim quote, 22 May), signaling material scale. However, no quantified value case: no cost reduction target, cycle-time improvement, error-rate savings, or ROI stated. Ammar's 'billions' reference positions the pain owner as senior procurement/supply chain, but the business case for THIS project (eProcurement + Intake + ANA) is unquantified. Gap: quantify the pain — what does disorganized intake cost TAQA in cycle time, maverick spend, or manual effort, and what is the target ROI for the $130k investment?

competition.narrative: Three competitors: (1) Oracle (incumbent, primary competitor per SF Competitor field) — TAQA is an ex-Zycus customer (2019–recent) who replaced Zycus with Oracle during a rolling deployment across countries (per Next Step 13 May: 'TAQA are an ex-customer we lost to Oracle, would be good to win them back'). Oracle incumbency is a wedge risk (residual skepticism, 'why come back?'), but no evidence of buyer satisfaction or Oracle renewal on THIS project (eProcurement + Intake + ANA scope). (2) GEP Worldwide and (3) JAGGAER (both named in SF Competitors__c field, no call/Next Step mention — likely from prior 2025 RFI that was shelved per Next Step 13 May). No active competitive evaluation surfaced on the 22 May call — no competitor named by buyer, no 'we are also looking at X' statement. Hamilton's framing ('give us a fair view of what your product is and how it fits... pros and cons') implies Zycus is sole vendor in THIS round, but buyer deciding whether to RFP — if RFP, GEP/JAGGAER/Oracle likely re-enter. How we win: (1) leverage TAQA relationship history (2019–recent, known entity) and position this as a win-back/expansion into Merlin AI (differentiated vs Oracle); (2) emphasize UAE local hosting (mandatory policy, confirmed 22 May) and Arabic language support (19+ languages, Oracle may not match); (3) lead with agentic AI (autonomous negotiation, Merlin Intake on Teams) as the wedge vs GEP/JAGGAER/Oracle — Zycus 'first to market' GenAI + Agentic AI, Gartner leader positioning; (4) lock Maximo integration as a technical fit (Tanya confirmed 'we can build the complete framework'); (5) fast-track to sole-source by quantifying ROI and securing EB sponsorship before RFP is issued (owner will qualify out if RFP, so avoid RFP path).

This is part of our policies and standard policies'), (2) IBM Maximo integration capability (TAQA uses Maximo for PR and PO lifecycle; Tanya confirmed 'we can build the complete framework' and redirect to Maximo), (3) ability to handle asset/project-linked PRs with bulk line items for strategic spares (Ammar: 'we are not a store to buy something very small and cheap, we are asset core companies dealing with billions'), and (4) support for both registered and unregistered vendors (Ammar asked if platform 'catches the cheapest price from the market, including our vendors and non-registered vendors'). Zycus positioned on all four. However, no formal evaluation scorecard, RFP criteria, or buyer-stated weighting. Hamilton framed the demo as giving Ammar 'a fair view of what your product is and how it fits' — exploratory, not structured eval. Gap: surface the formal decision criteria (if RFP proceeds, what are the eval pillars and weights?) and confirm whether the four points above are must-haves or nice-to-haves.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI Curious. Buyer engaged positively with Merlin AI demo (agentic negotiation, GenAI intake on Teams) on 22 May — Ammar confirmed understanding and resonated with tail-spend automation use case. However, AI was positioned by Zycus, not demanded by buyer. Ammar's questions focused on operational fit (UAE hosting, Maximo integration, asset PR handling, vendor management), not AI capability. No 'we need AI', 'AI is a priority', or innovation-agenda language from buyer. Pain is operational/process-oriented (disorganized intake, no guided buying), and AI is the tool Zycus offered to solve it — buyer is open but not AI-hungry. Tier: AI Curious (open to AI as a solution to stated pain, not actively seeking AI as a strategic mandate).

Next Step 2 Jun 2026 (27 days ago): Owner 'caught up with Rozario, next step is to gather feedback and decide whether to RFP — implied we may not respond as is a small opp, but let's keep talking just in case, especially if there is interest in the wider S2P — worth spending a little time on in case we can win back the TAQA logo, but if they go the RFX again we'll make a qualify in/out decision' (source: SF Next_Step__c). Owner rated confidence 5%.
No buyer reply since 2 Jun outreach (deal pulse: COOLING, rep-initiated outreach awaiting buyer reply, 27 days elapsed).

LastActivityDate 25 May 2026 (35 days ago) — no logged Task/Event since demo call. Single-threaded through Rozario (Influencer); Ammar engaged once but no follow-up; Hanan (Decision Maker) and Alia (Evaluator) silent.
north_star_verdict.headline: Discovery demo completed 22 May (~5 weeks ago), but buyer has gone quiet since rep outreach on 2 June (~4 weeks ago, awaiting reply). Owner rated confidence at 5% and flagged deal as 'maybe too small' — buyer deciding whether to RFP or deprioritize. No EB, no budget, single-threaded through Rozario. Win-back opportunity (ex-Zycus customer lost to Oracle) but need executive engagement to convert interest into committed project.

flags: Quantify AI value case before RFP decision: Ammar liked the ANA demo ('agent on top of procurement… negotiate and give best approach') but deal owner rated confidence at 5% and flagged 'maybe too small'. The single highest-leverage move is to co-create a savings model with Ammar: estimate TAQA Transmission's annual tail-spend PO volume (e.g. thousands of low-value PRs), apply ANA's negotiated savings per PO (e.g. 3–8% cost reduction per Zycus case studies), and model total annual savings (e.g. 'every dirham saved' × volume = material ROI). Present this to Ammar and the EB to justify budget and convert qualitative interest into a committed business case. Without this, buyer may decide project is 'too small' and deprioritize or qualify out.

Escalate to Economic Buyer to secure budget and block qualify-out: No EB identified; Ammar (Decision Maker role) is operational/technical but not budget authority. Buyer is deciding whether to RFP (2 Jun Next Step), and 24 days have passed with no reply—internal prioritization is unclear. The rank-1 move is for the deal owner to ask Rozario or Ammar to introduce him to the budget authority (likely Alia Alkaabi, Director Supply Chain Management, Evaluator role; or Ann-Marie Chapman, SVP Strategic Procurement; or Esmaeel Bustani, VP Procurement, both account-level contacts). An executive connect (deal owner's manager Carl Kimball + buyer VP/Director) may be warranted if deal size justifies (~$130k is borderline). Secure EB sponsorship before RFP decision to prevent qualify-out.

Co-author RFP criteria to lock in AI differentiation if buyer proceeds to formal eval: If buyer decides to RFP, GEP (peer S2P suite) and JAGGAER (mid-market alternative) will compete. Zycus positioned agentic AI (Merlin Intake, ANA) as differentiation on 22 May, but this has not been locked into buyer's evaluation criteria. The move is to work with Ammar (technical lead) and Hanan (procurement process lead) to draft or influence the RFP scorecard, weighting: (1) AI-driven tail-spend negotiation (ANA's autonomous RFQ/award vs manual process), (2) user experience/adoption (Merlin Intake on Teams vs traditional portal), (3) UAE-local hosting + IBM Maximo integration readiness. These are areas where Zycus leads and GEP/JAGGAER cannot match at same maturity. Shape the criteria before competitors lock in.
best_practice_check.summary: Single-threaded and cooling (Rozario Influencer sole active contact, Ammar engaged once, no EB/VP engagement). No quantified ROI (billions in strategic spend stated, but no cost reduction/cycle-time target for THIS $130k project). Owner confidence 5%, flagged 'maybe too small' — will qualify out if RFP. Oracle incumbency risk (win-back scenario, no evidence of buyer dissatisfaction). Close date 30 Sep not credible (93 days out, Qualified stage, no EB/budget/process).

competitive_position.summary: Three competitors: Oracle (incumbent, primary threat — TAQA replaced Zycus with Oracle 2019–recent, win-back scenario carries incumbency bias risk), GEP Worldwide and JAGGAER (both named in SF, likely from prior 2025 RFI that was shelved, no recent evidence). No active multi-vendor evaluation on the 22 May call — Zycus sole vendor in this round so far. Buyer deciding whether to RFP (Next Step 2 Jun); if RFP issued, GEP/JAGGAER/Oracle likely re-enter and owner will qualify out ('if they go the RFX again we'll make a qualify in/out decision'). Single strongest current threat: Oracle incumbency and the risk buyer defaults to Oracle expansion rather than switching back to Zycus — no evidence of buyer dissatisfaction with Oracle or regret about the switch. Wedge: position Merlin AI (agentic negotiation, GenAI intake on Teams) as the differentiated capability Oracle/GEP/JAGGAER cannot match, and fast-track to sole-source by securing EB sponsorship before RFP is issued.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strongly positioned on AI differentiation. Merlin AI (agentic negotiation, GenAI intake on Microsoft Teams) was the demo centerpiece on 22 May — Ammar confirmed understanding of autonomous negotiation agent ('your agent will be on top of our procurement models, negotiate with suppliers, give me the best approach') and resonated with the tail-spend use case. Zycus positioned as 'first to market' GenAI + Agentic AI in procurement, Gartner leader. However, AI appetite is INFERRED from demo engagement, not explicitly stated by buyer — no AIS score, no 'we need AI/Merlin' quote. Buyer pain is operational (disorganized intake, no guided buying, tail spend inefficiency), and AI is the solution Zycus offered, not a requirement buyer articulated. Risk: if buyer deprioritizes or defaults to Oracle expansion, AI wedge may not overcome incumbency bias. Under-positioned: buyer has not connected AI capability to a quantified business case (ROI on intake automation, tail-spend savings) — it is a feature demo, not a strategic imperative.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus positioned well on all four implied decision criteria from the 22 May demo call: (1) UAE data hosting (confirmed available, Ammar mandatory policy requirement met), (2) IBM Maximo integration (Tanya confirmed 'we can build the complete framework'), (3) asset/project PR handling for bulk strategic spares (Tanya confirmed platform can handle, system will show GCC vs foreign sourcing, registered/unregistered vendors), and (4) tail spend negotiation automation (autonomous negotiation agent demoed, resonated with Ammar).
